contextBlurb: Louisiana has 9 relevant bills tracked in 2025–2026 — 1 on data-center policy and 8 on AI regulation. 4 impose active bans or moratoriums.

legislation.title: Provides relative to permitting and reporting of water usage at data centers
legislation.summary: This Louisiana bill requires data centers to obtain permits and report on how much water they use. It creates new regulatory requirements aimed at tracking and managing the significant water consumption associated with data center cooling systems.

legislation.title: Establishes requirements for health insurance issuers using artificial intelligence or automated decision systems. (8/1/26)
legislation.summary: This Louisiana bill sets rules for health insurance companies that use AI or automated systems to make coverage and claims decisions. It aims to ensure these systems are transparent, accountable, and don't unfairly deny or limit patient benefits.

legislation.title: Prohibits the use of deepfake material against students enrolled in K-12. (8/1/26)
legislation.summary: This Louisiana law bans the creation or use of AI-generated fake images or videos (deepfakes) targeting K-12 students. It establishes enforceable protections for minors in schools against synthetic media abuse.

legislation.title: Provides relative to increases for certain utility rates
legislation.summary: This bill addresses utility rate increases for certain customers in Louisiana. It does not appear to be specifically focused on AI or data-center policy, though utility rates can affect both sectors.

legislation.title: Provides for lead hazard risk assessments for certain child care facilities and prekindergarten programs. (8/1/26) (RE INCREASE SG EX See Note)
legislation.summary: This bill requires lead hazard risk assessments in child care facilities and prekindergarten programs. It has no connection to AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Provides for the establishment of the Louisiana Center for Safe Schools within the Louisiana Commission on Law Enforcement and Administration of Criminal Justice (EG +$250,000 GF EX See Note)
legislation.summary: This bill establishes a Center for Safe Schools within Louisiana's law enforcement commission. It does not address AI or data-center policy.

legislation.title: Provides relative to information sharing for the purposes of Louisiana's Foundational Integrated Research System for Transformation (LA FIRST)
legislation.summary: This bill sets rules for how information can be shared within Louisiana's LA FIRST research and data integration system. It focuses on data sharing protocols for a state-run system aimed at transforming how Louisiana collects and uses public data.

legislation.title: Creates a consumer bill of rights regarding artificial intelligence
legislation.summary: This bill establishes a set of consumer rights related to artificial intelligence, giving people protections around how AI systems can affect them. It likely includes requirements for transparency, fairness, and accountability when businesses use AI to make decisions about consumers.

legislation.title: Provides relative to unlawful conduct involving images of another person created by artificial intelligence (RE SEE FISC NOTE GF EX)
legislation.summary: This Louisiana bill makes it illegal to create or share AI-generated images of real people without their consent. It establishes criminal penalties for those who use artificial intelligence to produce unauthorized or harmful depictions of individuals.

legislation.title: Requires disclosure of the use of artificial intelligence in telephone campaign communications
legislation.summary: This bill requires political campaigns to disclose when artificial intelligence is used to create or assist in telephone communications. Voters must be informed when AI-generated content is used in campaign calls.

legislation.title: Prohibits public contracts with entities owned or controlled by foreign adversaries for the provision of artificial intelligence technology
legislation.summary: This bill bans Louisiana government agencies from signing contracts with companies owned or controlled by foreign adversaries to provide AI technology. It is designed to protect state systems from potential foreign influence or security risks through AI tools.

legislation.title: Prohibits using artificial intelligence to create child sexual abuse materials. (8/1/26)
legislation.summary: This bill bans the use of AI to generate child sexual abuse material. Violations are subject to criminal prohibition under Louisiana law.
